company_type: Technology - Data Infrastructure and Storage Solutions

manufacturing_locations: United States (Fremont, California; San Jose, California)
supply_chain.dependencies: Limited number of suppliers for drives and other components; reliance on third-party contract manufacturers; single-source suppliers for certain components; consolidation among suppliers in semiconductor and storage media industries
risks: Limited control over manufacturing operations outsourced to third parties
Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical disputes, acts of terrorism, cyber attacks
Natural disasters (floods, earthquakes, storms, fires) especially from climate change
Labor and political unrest at facilities not operated or owned by NetApp
Business, regulatory compliance, legal compliance issues affecting suppliers
Supply constraints and price increases from limited supplier base
Dependence on limited number of suppliers for critical components (NAND, drives)
Supplier consolidation leading to price volatility and supply constraints
Potential for suppliers to allocate volumes to competitors during constraints

regulatory_risks: U.S. government spending uncertainty and budget constraints
Compliance with U.S. government regulatory and contractual requirements (pricing, cybersecurity, procurement integrity)
False Claims Act and other statute violations risk
Government certification requirements changes (CMMC, EO 14028, Section 5949 of FY23 NDAA)
Export control laws and regulations (Export Administration Regulations, OFAC sanctions)
Restrictions on sale/export to certain countries, entities, persons (Russia, Belarus, Ukraine regions)
Data privacy and protection laws (GDPR, CCPA, California Privacy Rights Act, state-based regulations)
Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) for EU financial institutions
Environmental regulations (REACH, ErP, RoHS, WEEE, EPR regulations)
Human trafficking supply chain regulations (U.S., UK, Australia)
BEPS Pillar Two global minimum tax framework
Potential AI regulation and compliance requirements
Intellectual property laws varying by jurisdiction
Antitrust and anti-competition laws in U.S. and foreign jurisdictions
Tax law changes and OECD BEPS implementation

sanction_risks: U.S. government sanctions on Russia, Belarus, and regions of Ukraine
Economic sanctions and export controls impacting product sales and service
Potential retaliatory trade actions from countries targeted by U.S. tariffs
Risk of nationalization of foreign businesses in Russia
Currency restrictions and regulations in sanctioned countries
U.S.-China trade tensions and investment restrictions (Outbound Investment Security Program)
Tensions between China and Taiwan affecting supply chain component sourcing
Tariffs and trade barriers (recent U.S. tariffs on certain products and countries, potential future tariffs)
Export restrictions on semiconductor products and services to certain countries
